800	/// Convert regex captures to a dictionary.
801	fn captures_to_dict(cap: regex::Captures) -> Dict {
802	    let m = cap.get(0).expect("missing first match");
803	    dict! {
804	        "start" => m.start(),
805	        "end" => m.end(),
806	        "text" => m.as_str(),
807	        "captures" =>  cap.iter()
808	            .skip(1)
809	            .map(|opt| opt.map_or(Value::None, |m| m.as_str().into_value()))
810	            .collect::<Array>(),
811	    }
812	}
813

817	    eco_format!("string index out of bounds (index: {}, len: {})", index, len)

823	    eco_format!("no default value was specified and string index out of bounds (index: {}, len: {})", index, len)
824	}

830	}

content.text:
Implemented the `case` parameter for `str.first()` and `str.last()` in `crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs`:

- Added an optional `#[named] case: Option<StrCase>` parameter to both methods.
- Added a new `StrCase` enum (`Upper`/`Lower`/`Title`) deriving `Cast`, which maps to the string values `"upper"`, `"lower"`, `"title"` (invalid strings raise Typst's standard descriptive cast error automatically).
- Added an `apply_case` helper: uppercase/lowercase use Rust's Unicode-aware `to_uppercase`/`to_lowercase`; title case uppercases the first scalar value and lowercases the rest, per spec.
- When `case` is omitted, behavior is unchanged.

Verified logic by reading the `Cast` derive macro (confirms kebab-case string mapping) and checked `Str`'s `From<String>`/`From<&str>` impls. No `cargo` available in this sandbox to compile-check. Diff written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`.

result:
Implemented the `case` parameter for `str.first()` and `str.last()` in `crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs`:

- Added an optional `#[named] case: Option<StrCase>` parameter to both methods.
- Added a new `StrCase` enum (`Upper`/`Lower`/`Title`) deriving `Cast`, which maps to the string values `"upper"`, `"lower"`, `"title"` (invalid strings raise Typst's standard descriptive cast error automatically).
- Added an `apply_case` helper: uppercase/lowercase use Rust's Unicode-aware `to_uppercase`/`to_lowercase`; title case uppercases the first scalar value and lowercases the rest, per spec.
- When `case` is omitted, behavior is unchanged.

Verified logic by reading the `Cast` derive macro (confirms kebab-case string mapping) and checked `Str`'s `From<String>`/`From<&str>` impls. No `cargo` available in this sandbox to compile-check. Diff written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`.
